Cocc..Back, pain: The Cocculus headaches and backaches, pains and distress are present.
Cocc..Chest: The heart is weak, pulse feeble.
Cocc..Chill: Intermittent fevers with pains in the limbs, especially in the knees and bones of the legs, with that peculiar stiffness, nausea, and loathing of food.
Cocc..Extremities: Paralytic weakness in the limbs, numbness, jerking of the muscles, twitching, quivering, loss of sensation, loss of power, muscular weakness in all the limbs. Numbness and paralytic feeling in the limbs.
Cocc..Extremities, paralysis: Paralysis of the lower extremities, proceeding from the small of the back. Arising from cold, from the abuse of Mercury. Paralysis of the lower limbs, with stiffness, numbness and bruised feeling.
Cocc..Extremities, paralysis, painless: It was the case of a little girl with paralysis of lower extremities after diphtheria and no hope was given. But Doctor Moore (he was then an Octogenarian) looked over the case.
Cocc..Generalities: Cocculus slows down all the activities of the body and mind, producing a sort of paralytic weakness. Behind time in all its actions.
Cocc..Genitalia female: Copious menstrual flow, menses too soon; last too long. Catamenia two weeks before the time. In women prostrated from grief and from anxiety, and from prolonged loss of sleep, menses come too soon, are copious and prolonged.
Cocc..Genitalia female, pain: Violent, cramping pains in the bowels, clutching pains in the uterus during menstruation.
Cocc..Head: Congestion of the head, pressing, throbbing headache. Headache as if the skull would burst, or like a great valve opening and shutting. Sick headache with vertigo.
Cocc..Mind: You can hardly see what is coming when the mental activities are slowed down, from anxiety, and loss of sleep, such as we have in nursing. The mind appears like approaching imbecility, and as you look upon the true Cocculus case you wonder if that patient has not been growing insane for a year or two, because the mind seems almost a blank.
Cocc..Mouth: Metallic taste in the mouth. Bitter taste in the mouth. Sour, nauseous taste in the mouth, and no food tempts him.
Cocc..Rectum: A paralytic condition of the rectum. Inability to press at stool. Urging to stool and burning in rectum.
Cocc..Rectum, constipation: Disposition to stool, but peristaltic motion in upper intestines is wanting.
Cocc..Sleep: Sleeplessness from long nursing and from night watching; that is a symptom that I have called your attention to so often. Anxious, frightful dreams; ill effects from loss of sleep and night watching.
Cocc..Sleep, restless: Slightest loss of sleep tells on him.
Cocc..Stomach: Spasms of the stomach. Violent attacks of gastralgia, violent cramp of the stomach. Griping, pinching, constrictive pain.
Cocc..Stomach, pain: Violent attacks of gastralgia, violent cramp of the stomach. Griping, pinching, constrictive pain.
Cocc..Vertigo: The vertigo is visually attended with nausea. A Cocculus case cannot look out of the car window, cannot look down from the boat and see water moving, without nausea immediately.
Cocc..Abdomen, pain: The pain in the bowels feels as if the intestines were pinched between sharp stones. This causes fainting and vomiting. Colicky pains in the bowels great distension of the abdomen. Tearing, cutting, spasmodic pains in the bowels.
Cocc..Back, weakness: She is exhausted; unable to sustain any mental or physical effort; weak in the knees, weak in the back.
Cocc..Generalities, faintness: Faintness on moving the body, fainting from pain in the bowels, from colic.
Cocc..Generalities, pain: With all this slowing down of the thoughts and activities the patient remains extremely sensitive to suffering, sensitive to pain.
Cocc..Clinical, paralysis: I remember a case of paralysis of the lower extremities that was prescribed for by a very careful homeopathic physician many years ago. He studied the case carefully and gave Cocculus c.m. It was not many days before the child began to move the legs, and the condition was perfectly cleared up.
Cocc..Generalities, weakness: He cannot endure any muscular exertion, because he is weak; he is tired. First comes this slowness, then a sort of visible paralytic condition, and then complete paralysis.
